critic_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. A music streaming service developed a two-stage recommendation stack...

CRITIQUE:

1. [SUPERVISION-SOURCE-MISMATCH] Impression-level training with skip/non-consumption labels vs. positive-play-only training. These are different tasks, not different architectures on the same task.
2. [FEATURE-SET-MISMATCH] Audio-content embeddings and skip behavior in neural retriever vs. play events only in baseline. Fundamental feature set asymmetry.
3. [NO-CONTENT-AWARE-BASELINE] No impression-aware MF or content-aware shallow model. No way to isolate the neural architecture contribution.
defender_raw:
You are reviewing an ML project. A music streaming service developed a two-stage recommendation stack...

DEFENSE:

This is a system-level comparison against the incumbent. The new retriever's additional features and richer supervision are part of the system's design. The result demonstrates system-level improvement. The online experiment next step will validate real-world impact.
adjudication_raw: System-level improvement vs. incumbent is a valid operational claim. But the paper claims to demonstrate 'clear improvement in candidate generation quality' as a scientific contribution. That claim requires input and supervision parity. The paper's own description confirms the supervision and feature differences. Critique wins.
